vocab.meaning: trở nên dạn dĩ, cởi mở hơn
vocab.example: I used to be quite introverted, but I've recently tried to come out of my shell by initiating brief chats with people at bus stops.

vocab.meaning: có cùng tần số, suy nghĩ giống nhau
vocab.example: It was surprising how quickly I realized that a stranger I met at the park was on the same wavelength as me regarding environmental issues.
While waiting in a long queue, I decided to strike up a conversation with the person behind me to pass the time.

vocab.meaning: người hướng ngoại, giỏi giao thiệp
vocab.example: I wouldn't exactly call myself a social butterfly, but I do enjoy the occasional spontaneous encounter with a stranger.

vocab.word: get off on the right foot
vocab.meaning: có một khởi đầu thuận lợi (trong một cuộc gặp gỡ)
vocab.example: If you share a genuine compliment, you usually get off on the right foot with someone you’ve never met before.

vocab.meaning: khả năng ăn nói lưu loát và thuyết phục
vocab.example: My friend has the gift of the gab; he can talk his way into a deep discussion with anyone, from taxi drivers to CEOs.

vocab.meaning: phá cách, làm điều gì đó khác biệt so với thói quen thường ngày
vocab.example: Talking to people in public helps me break the mould of my usual routine and see the world through someone else's eyes.

vocab.meaning: tán gẫu, trò chuyện phiếm về những thứ không quan trọng
vocab.example: I spent about twenty minutes shooting the breeze with an elderly man at the library about the history of the city.
Even with a complete stranger, it’s often quite easy to find common ground if you both enjoy the same sports team.

vocab.meaning: gây ra một tình huống rắc rối hoặc tranh cãi (thường dùng để nói về việc tránh các chủ đề nhạy cảm)
vocab.example: I love chatting with strangers, but I avoid politics because I don't want to accidentally open a can of worms.
